notes: Taxonomy and page order extracted from Skill Finder index pages. Skill levels (Basic, Intermediate A/B, Advanced) extracted from detail pages where photographed (pp. 103–116). riseLinks populated separately when wiring to RISE cards.

skills.name: V-Sit
skills.description: Sit on the floor and slide the feet toward the buttocks (tuck position), with the hands on the floor next to the buttocks. Lift the legs to a V position, hold momentarily, and then lift the hands to an extended position sideward while the legs are still in the V position. Balance for two seconds.
keyPoints: Hands beside buttocks to begin
Lift legs to a V then release hands sideward
Hold balance for two seconds
photoSequence: Seated tuck position, hands on floor beside hips, legs raised in V-shape, body balanced on tailbone

skills.name: Front Support
skills.description: From a prone position, push up on the hands and the balls of the feet, with the body in a straight line. Also known as a push-up position.
keyPoints: Body forms a straight line from head to heels
Weight on hands and balls of feet
photoSequence: Straight plank position supported on hands and toes, body rigid and horizontal

skills.name: Rear Support
skills.description: From a sitting position, push up on the hands (hands facing outward or toward the buttocks) and feet, with the body in a straight line.
keyPoints: Hands can face outward or toward buttocks
Body in a straight line from head to heels
Weight on hands and heels
photoSequence: Reverse plank position, body elevated in straight line, weight on hands and heels, face up

skills.name: Side Support
skills.description: Lie on one side, stack the feet, and place a bent elbow underneath the shoulder. Lift up from the hips onto the elbow and edge of the bottom foot (not the ankle), forming a straight line with the body.
keyPoints: Elbow directly under shoulder
Lift from hips — do not sag
Support on elbow and edge of bottom foot, not ankle
Body forms a straight line
photoSequence: Side plank on elbow: body in straight diagonal line from head to stacked feet, top arm along body

skills.name: Lunge
skills.description: Step one foot forward to a stand in a lunge position, with the knee of the front leg flexed. The head is up with eyes focused forward, the shoulders and hips are square, the back leg is straight, both feet are turned out slightly, the arms are stretched upward by the ears, and slightly more weight is over the front leg than the back leg.
keyPoints: Front knee flexed over toes
Back leg straight
Shoulders and hips square
Arms stretched upward by ears
Eyes focused forward
Feet turned out slightly
Slightly more weight over front leg
photoSequence: Lunge position: front knee bent, back leg straight, arms stretched high by ears, upright torso, eyes forward

skills.name: Arabesque
skills.description: Stand on both feet, keeping the upper body vertical and extended. Lift one leg backward and upward (6 to 12 inches [15 to 30 cm] or higher), maintaining a vertical curve in the body. Arms are stretched out to the sides or low near the body.
keyPoints: Upper body vertical and extended
Lifted leg goes backward and upward, 6–12 inches minimum
Maintain a vertical curve through the body
Arms to sides or low near body
photoSequence: Arabesque: standing on one leg, other leg extended backward and upward, arms out to sides, upright torso

skills.name: Scale
skills.description: In a scale, the upper body is lowered (shoulders to about hip height) as the back leg is raised to horizontal or above. Arms are stretched out to the sides or low near the body.
keyPoints: Upper body lowers as back leg rises — they counterbalance
Aim for back leg at or above horizontal
Arms stretched to sides or low near body
photoSequence: Scale: one leg raised to horizontal or beyond, upper body tipped forward to match, arms out to sides, forming a T-shape

skills.name: Side Scale
skills.description: Stand on both feet. Lift one leg sideward while simultaneously lowering the upper body to the opposite side. Try to hold the leg at or above horizontal. Arms can be stretched to the sides or low near the body.
keyPoints: Leg lifts sideward as body tilts to opposite side
Hold leg at or above horizontal
Arms to sides or low near body
photoSequence: Side scale: one leg raised sideward, upper body leaning to opposite side, arms extended

skills.name: Front-Leg Balance
skills.description: Keeping the base leg straight, raise one leg forward and upward to a 45-degree angle or higher. Maintain a straight vertical position with the trunk of the body. The lifted leg should be straight and turned out slightly. Arms can be stretched to the sides or low near the body.
keyPoints: Base leg stays straight
Lifted leg forward to at least 45 degrees
Keep trunk vertical
Lifted leg straight and slightly turned out
photoSequence: Front-leg balance: standing on one straight leg, other leg raised forward at 45+ degrees, trunk vertical, arms at sides

skills.name: Cross-Legged Hand Support
skills.description: Sit cross-legged with the hands on the floor by the hips. Lift to a support with the buttocks and legs off the floor by extending the arms (the feet do not need to come off the floor on the first few attempts).
keyPoints: Hands placed beside hips
Extend arms to lift hips
Feet need not leave floor initially
photoSequence: Cross-legged sit with hands on floor beside hips, arms extended, hips lifted off floor

skills.name: Tuck Support
skills.description: Sit in a tuck position (feet slid toward the buttocks) with the hands on the floor by the hips. Lift to a support by extending the arms and attempting to lift the buttocks and feet off the floor.
keyPoints: Tuck position to start — feet drawn toward buttocks
Hands by hips
Extend arms to lift bodyweight
Aim to get buttocks and feet off floor
photoSequence: Tuck sit with hands on floor, arms extended lifting hips and tucked legs off floor

skills.name: Half L-Support
skills.description: Perform the tuck support, and then attempt to slowly extend one leg into a pike position, keeping the other leg in a tuck position. Repeat the activity using the other leg.
keyPoints: Start from tuck support
Slowly extend one leg to pike
Other leg remains tucked
Repeat on both sides
photoSequence: L-support with one leg extended straight, other leg tucked, weight on hands, hips lifted

skills.name: L-Support
skills.description: Sit in an open pike position (legs straight out in front) with the hands on the floor by the hips. Extend the arms to lift the buttocks and legs off the floor in the open pike position while trying to maintain a vertical position of the torso.
keyPoints: Start in open pike sit
Hands by hips
Extend arms to lift hips and legs
Keep legs straight
Try to keep torso vertical
photoSequence: Full L-support: both legs straight and parallel to floor, weight on hands, torso upright, body making an L-shape

skills.name: Tripod
skills.description: From a squat position, form a triangle with the hands and head (the head is at the top of the triangle; the head touches the floor at the hairline). Place one knee on each elbow, and lift the hips to balance on the hands and forehead.
keyPoints: Head and hands form a triangle on the floor
Head contacts floor at the hairline
Knees rest on elbows
Hips lift to balance
photoSequence: Tripod balance: knees resting on elbows, forehead on floor, hips raised, weight on hands and head

skills.name: Double-Knee Uprise
skills.description: From a tripod position, slowly lift the legs to a tuck position and hold. The majority of the body weight should be supported on the hands.
keyPoints: Begin in tripod
Slowly lift both knees to tuck
Transfer weight to hands
Hold position
photoSequence: Inverted tuck balance: knees drawn up toward chest, weight shifting to hands, head still lightly on floor

skills.name: Headstand
skills.description: Balance in a tripod, and then extend both the hips and legs to balance in an extended headstand. Support and balance are provided by the arms. The body is extended, showing control and balance.
keyPoints: Start from tripod
Extend hips and legs upward
Arms provide support and balance
Body fully extended in line
photoSequence: Headstand: body fully extended vertically, weight on hands and head, legs together and straight pointing upward

skills.name: Headstand Roll-Out
skills.description: Balance in a headstand position. After balancing, allow the legs to lean slightly forward, push with the hands to lift the head slightly off the floor, tuck the head, and roll forward through a squat position to stand.
keyPoints: Start from headstand
Lean legs slightly forward to initiate
Push hands to lift head off floor
Tuck chin and roll forward
Finish through squat to stand
photoSequence: Headstand: body fully extended, balanced vertically
Legs begin to tip forward, arms push to lift head clear
Tucked forward roll in progress, hips passing over

skills.name: Lever
skills.description: From a stand with arms high, step forward, moving through a lunge. Place the hands on the floor, and lift the back leg as high as possible while keeping the support leg on the floor. Lift the hands from the floor back to a lunge position.
keyPoints: Step forward through a lunge
Hands go to floor momentarily
Lift back leg as high as possible
Support leg remains on floor
Lift hands back to return to lunge
photoSequence: Lunge forward, arms high, body leaning into move
Hands on floor, back leg raised as high as possible, forming an inverted scale

skills.name: Single-Leg Uprise Toward Handstand
skills.description: From a stand with arms high, step forward, moving through a lunge. Place the hands on the floor, and lift the back leg as high as possible. Keep the arms extended. Push off the base leg so both feet are in the air (legs do not need to come together in the air). Lower the base leg first, and push off the hands back to a lunge position.
keyPoints: Step forward into lunge
Hands on floor, arms extended
Kick back leg high
Push off base leg — both feet briefly airborne
Lower base leg first then push off hands
skills.spotting: Spotter may assist at the hips.
photoSequence: Lunge forward, arms high, preparing to kick
Hands on floor, back leg kicked high, base leg pushing off — both feet in air

skills.name: Three-Quarter Handstand, Switch Legs
skills.description: From a stand with arms high, step forward, moving through a lunge. Place the hands on the floor, and lift the back leg as high as possible. Keep the arms extended. Push off the base leg, and switch leg positions in the air, lowering the first leg. Push off the hands back to a lunge position.
keyPoints: Same entry as single-leg uprise
Switch leg positions while airborne
Lower original back leg first
Push off hands to return to lunge
photoSequence: Lunge entry, arms high
Hands on floor, back leg kicks high, base leg pushing off
Leg switch in inverted position — legs pass each other in the air

skills.name: Walk the Wall
skills.description: From a prone position with feet against the wall, walk the feet part way up the wall while simultaneously moving the hands closer to the wall. Then slowly reverse the process to return to a prone position. Strength will determine how far up the wall the gymnast can go.
keyPoints: Start prone with feet at base of wall
Walk feet up as hands walk in
Reverse to come down
Strength determines height achieved
photoSequence: Prone position, feet against wall base, hands on floor
Feet walking up wall, hands moving closer to wall, body approaching vertical
Near-handstand position against wall, body inverted

skills.name: Handstand
skills.description: From a stand with arms high, step forward to pass through a lunge. Lift the back leg while placing the hands on the floor shoulder-width apart, and push off the front leg. Bring the legs together in a straight handstand position. The head is neutral. Hold momentary balance. Step down and raise the chest to finish in a lunge.
keyPoints: Step forward through lunge
Hands shoulder-width apart on floor
Push off front leg, lift back leg
Bring legs together in straight line
Head neutral
Hold momentary balance
Step down and raise chest to lunge finish
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended. Assist by lifting the hips and, if necessary, steadying the legs when the gymnast kicks to handstand. Never hold the gymnast by the ankles.
photoSequence: Lunge forward, arms raised, about to kick
Hands on floor, back leg kicked, front leg pushing off
Full handstand — legs together, body straight, arms extended

skills.name: Handstand Roll From Wall
skills.description: Walk the feet up the wall to a hollow handstand position, with the abdomen facing the wall. Push off the wall with the feet, tuck the head, and roll forward on the back to a stand. Arms remain as straight as possible and the shoulder angle is extended to begin the roll. The head should not bear any weight on the roll. Show control and balance.
keyPoints: Walk up wall to hollow handstand, abdomen facing wall
Push off wall with feet
Tuck head to initiate forward roll
Keep arms as straight as possible
Head bears no weight during roll
Shoulder angle extended to start roll
skills.spotting: An additional mat or skill cushion can be used, and spotting is recommended on the first few attempts.
photoSequence: Hollow handstand with abdomen to wall, feet on wall
Feet push off wall, body begins to tip forward

skills.name: Bridge Over High Edge
skills.description: Lie in a supine position with the head toward the high edge of an incline (wedge) mat. Reach the hands overhead. Place the hands on the floor with fingertips pointing toward the mat and as close to the edge of the wedge as possible. Bridge up to support. Show strong, straight arms and extension through the shoulders and upper back in the bridge position (there should be little to no arch in the lower back). With the chest stretching over the fingertips, push off with one leg while lifting the other leg overhead (walkover position), and step down to the floor.
keyPoints: Head toward high edge of wedge
Fingertips pointing toward mat, close to edge
Strong straight arms in bridge
Extension through shoulders and upper back — minimal lower back arch
Push off one leg, lift other overhead to walkover
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended. Assist by lifting the gymnast's hips. Be sure the gymnast is strong enough to support herself in the bridge position.
photoSequence: Coach supporting gymnast in bridge position at high edge of wedge mat
Gymnast beginning walkover, one leg raised overhead

skills.name: Stretch Jump
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on Two Feet
skills.description: From a stand on two feet with heels on the floor, bend the knees slightly (demi-plié), arms at the sides. Simultaneously push off the floor by extending through the hips, knees, ankles, and toes and raise the arms forward and upward. While in the air, maintain a stretched (straight) body, with arms by the ears and legs straight and together. Land on two feet, bending the knees slightly and lowering the arms to front middle (safe landing position).
keyPoints: Begin with demi-plié, arms at sides
Push through hips, knees, ankles and toes simultaneously
Arms swing forward and upward
In air: body stretched, arms by ears, legs together
Land on two feet with bent knees
Arms lower to front middle on landing
photoSequence: Standing start, knees in slight demi-plié, arms at sides
Airborne: body fully stretched, arms by ears, legs together and pointed
Landing: feet together, knees bending, arms lowering to front middle

skills.name: Tuck Jump
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on Two Feet
skills.description: Begin as for a stretch jump. As soon as the feet leave the floor, bend both knees forward and upward to a minimum 90-degree hip and knee angle. Before landing, extend the hips and legs to a stretched position. Land in a safe landing position with the arms in front middle position.
keyPoints: Same takeoff as stretch jump
Immediately tuck both knees forward and upward on leaving floor
Minimum 90-degree hip and knee angle
Extend fully before landing
Safe landing position
photoSequence: Airborne: knees tucked tightly to chest, arms out for balance
Extending legs before landing

skills.name: Split Jump
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on Two Feet
skills.description: Begin as for a stretch jump. As soon as the feet leave the floor, separate the legs into a stride split position to a minimum of 30 degrees. Before landing, bring the legs together. Land on two feet in a safe landing position. Arms can be raised or at side middle during the jump.
keyPoints: Stretch jump takeoff
Separate legs into stride split — minimum 30 degrees
Bring legs together before landing
Arms raised or at side middle
photoSequence: Airborne: legs split forward and backward, arms raised or to sides

skills.name: Straddle Jump
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on Two Feet
skills.description: Begin as for a stretch jump. As soon as the feet leave the floor, separate the legs to a straddle position (right leg extended to the right and left leg extended to the left). Before landing, bring the legs together. Land on two feet in a safe landing position. Arms can be raised or at front middle during the jump.
keyPoints: Stretch jump takeoff
Legs separate sideward into straddle
Bring legs together before landing
Arms raised or at front middle
photoSequence: Airborne: both legs extended sideward in wide straddle, arms raised

skills.name: Bounding
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on Two Feet
skills.description: From a stand on two feet, perform a low stretch jump. Upon landing, with little flexion of the hips, knees, or ankles, quickly push off and jump again. Repeat three to five times. Arms should remain up by the ears. After the final jump, finish in a safe landing position.
keyPoints: Low stretch jumps repeated in series
Minimal flexion at hips, knees, ankles on landing — rebound quickly
Arms remain up by ears throughout
3–5 repetitions
Finish in safe landing position
photoSequence: Standing start, arms overhead
Low jump — arms by ears, legs straight
Landing with minimal knee bend, immediately rebounding

skills.name: Combination Jumps
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on Two Feet
skills.description: To add variety and difficulty, practice combination jumps by connecting two or three jumps together in a series. Start with the same jump repeated, and then advance to different jumps in a series. For example, a sequence could be tuck jump and pike jump.
keyPoints: Connect 2–3 jumps in sequence
Start with same jump repeated
Progress to different jump types in series
photoSequence: Standing start, arms overhead
First jump airborne — tuck position
Landing and immediate rebound
Second jump airborne — pike position

skills.name: Hopping
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on One Foot
skills.description: Standing on one leg, perform a low stretch jump and land on the same foot. Repeat several times in succession, and then switch legs. Arms can be in various positions: stretched high, low to the sides, low to the front, or side middle.
keyPoints: Take off and land on the same foot
Low stretch jump action
Repeat several times then switch legs
Arms can vary position
photoSequence: Standing on one leg, knee slightly bent
Airborne on one foot, other leg raised, arms varied

skills.name: Assemblé
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on One Foot
skills.description: Lift one leg forward and upward, then push off the other leg and bring both legs together in the air. Finish by landing on both feet in a controlled position. Repeat the activity, beginning with the other leg.
keyPoints: Lift one leg forward and upward first
Push off standing leg
Bring legs together in air
Land on both feet in controlled position
Repeat on both sides
photoSequence: One leg lifts forward, standing leg bends to push off
Both legs come together in the air
Two-foot landing in controlled position

skills.name: Hitch Kick
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on One Foot
skills.description: Step forward and push off the back foot while swinging the front leg forward and upward. Switch leg positions in the air, and land on the front leg. Arms can be at side middle or stretched high.
keyPoints: Step forward, push off back foot
Swing front leg forward and upward
Switch leg positions in air
Land on the original front leg
Arms side middle or stretched high
photoSequence: Step forward, front leg swinging up, arms raised
Legs switching in air — scissor kick at height
Landing on leading leg with control

skills.name: Stride Leap
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on One Foot
skills.description: From a slow run forward, push off one foot to rise in the air, separate the legs to a stride (straight split) position, and land on the front foot with the knee slightly flexed. Repeat the activity, pushing off the other leg. Arms are in opposition to the legs.
keyPoints: Slow run approach
Push off one foot to rise
Separate legs to full stride/split position
Land on front foot, knee slightly flexed
Arms in opposition to legs
photoSequence: Run approach, push-off foot extending
Airborne: legs in full split position, arms in opposition

skills.name: Sissone
skills.subcategory: Jumping From and Landing on One Foot
skills.description: Step forward, and move the back foot forward to a position behind the front foot. Jump from both feet, and separate the legs to a stride position. Land on the front leg in a low arabesque. Arms can be stretched high, at side middle, or in opposition. Repeat the move on the other side.
keyPoints: Step, bring back foot behind front
Jump from both feet
Separate legs to stride in air
Land on front leg in low arabesque
Repeat on both sides
photoSequence: Step forward, back foot drawing in behind
Airborne: legs in stride split
Landing on front leg in low arabesque position

skills.name: Pivot Turn
skills.description: Step forward on relevé with the right foot and then quickly close the left foot behind the right, maintaining the relevé position. Turn quickly to the left 180 degrees in the relevé position. Lower the heels to the floor. Arms can be on the hips or in crown position (curved overhead).
keyPoints: Step forward on relevé
Close back foot quickly behind
Turn 180 degrees on relevé
Lower heels to floor to finish
Arms on hips or crown position
photoSequence: Step forward, rising to relevé
Facing sideward mid-turn, feet in relevé
Completed 180-degree turn facing new direction

skills.name: Turn (Pirouette)
skills.description: Step forward, quickly rising up on the ball of the front foot, and turn 90 degrees, 180 degrees, or 360 degrees to finish in various positions. Typically, a turn is performed with the arms raised in a crown position and the big toe of the free leg touching the knee of the support leg; however, arm and free-leg positions can vary.
keyPoints: Step forward, rise on ball of front foot
Turn 90, 180, or 360 degrees
Arms in crown position (typically)
Big toe of free leg to knee of support leg (typically)
Arm and leg positions can vary
photoSequence: Step forward, beginning to rise on ball of foot
Mid-turn: on one foot, free leg at knee, arms in crown
Completion in various finish positions

skills.name: Log Roll
skills.description: Using an incline (wedge) mat, begin in a supine position on the upper end of the wedge, with the body fully extended. Roll over into a prone position, and continue rolling to the end of the wedge. Repeat the activity, rolling back up the wedge and then rolling in the opposite direction. Log rolls can also be performed on the floor.
keyPoints: Body fully extended throughout
Roll from supine to prone and continue
Can roll on wedge or flat floor
Practice in both directions
photoSequence: Supine extended position on wedge, arms overhead
Rolling sideways, body passing through side-lying
Prone position, continuing to roll down slope

skills.name: Stretch Jump With Twist
skills.description: Jump up from the floor with the body in an extended (stretched) position, twist 180 degrees or 360 degrees, and land on both feet. Repeat the jump, turning in the opposite direction. Arms should be either stretched straight above the head or in a softer crown position.
keyPoints: Stretch jump takeoff
Twist 180 or 360 degrees
Land on both feet
Repeat in opposite direction
Arms stretched above head or in crown position
photoSequence: Takeoff: body extended, arms raising
Airborne: body in stretched twist, arms controlling rotation
Landing: two feet, facing new direction

skills.name: Slide Off Incline, Forward Roll
skills.description: Begin in a prone position, with the head toward the high end of the wedge; slide forward, placing the hands on the floor next to the wedge. With support on the arms, look at the belly and roll over forward off the wedge and onto the floor. Hands should be placed as close to the edge of the wedge as possible. Arms should be straight and reaching at an angle forward and up at the end of the skill.
keyPoints: Head toward high end of wedge
Slide forward, hands go to floor beside wedge
Look at belly to initiate roll
Hands as close to wedge edge as possible
Arms straight reaching forward and up at finish
photoSequence: Prone on wedge, head at high end, sliding forward
Hands on floor as body tips over edge, tucked forward roll onto mat

skills.name: Tip Over Incline, Forward Roll
skills.description: Begin on the shins, positioned on the high end of the wedge facing away from the down slope. Arms are extended high. Reach for the floor with the hands, look at the belly, and roll over forward to finish in a standing position on the floor. Hands should be placed as close to the edge of the wedge as possible. Arms should be as straight as possible and reaching forward and up at an angle at the end of the skill.
keyPoints: Start kneeling on high end of wedge, facing away from slope
Arms extended high
Reach hands to floor, look at belly
Hands close to edge of wedge
Arms straight at finish, reaching forward and up
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended. Assist by lifting the hips.
photoSequence: Coach supporting gymnast kneeling on wedge, arms high
Gymnast rolling forward off wedge, coach guiding hips

skills.name: Straddle Forward Roll Down Incline
skills.description: Stand on the high edge of the wedge in a straddle position, facing down the wedge. Reach between and behind the legs, and place the hands on the mat to perform a forward roll to stand with feet together. Arms reach forward and up at the end of the skill. Place the upper back on the wedge at the beginning of the roll. The roll can also be done to straddle stand.
keyPoints: Straddle stand on high edge, facing down slope
Reach between and behind legs for hand placement
Forward roll to feet-together stand
Upper back contacts wedge at start of roll
Arms reach forward and up at end
photoSequence: Straddle stand at top of wedge, hands reaching between legs
Rolling forward down wedge, tucked position

skills.name: Tuck Forward Roll Down Incline
skills.description: From a standing position, squat down on the high end of the wedge, facing down the wedge. Place the hands on the wedge in front of the feet, shoulder-width apart. Raise the hips, tuck the head, and push off the feet to perform a tuck forward roll down the wedge, finishing in a straight stand. Arms are stretched up by the ears or slightly forward at the end of the skill. Maintain the tuck position throughout the roll, and stand up without using the hands to push on the mat.
keyPoints: Squat at high end, hands shoulder-width on wedge
Raise hips, tuck head, push off feet
Tuck position maintained throughout roll
Finish in straight stand without pushing off hands
Arms by ears or forward at end
photoSequence: Squat position at top of wedge, hands placed forward
Tucked roll down wedge, hips overhead

skills.name: Roll Up and Over Incline
skills.description: Step onto the low end of the wedge, facing the up slope. Moving through a scale position, roll over forward up the wedge to a sitting position on the high edge of the wedge, with arms forward.
keyPoints: Start at low end, face up slope
Move through a scale position
Roll over forward up the wedge
Finish sitting at top with arms forward
photoSequence: Scale position on wedge, one leg raised behind
Rolling forward up the slope, body tucked

skills.name: Roll Over and Down Incline
skills.description: Facing the high end of the wedge, place the hands on the mat, jump off both feet to a forward roll down the wedge, finishing in a stand. Arms are stretched up by the ears at the end of the skill. Control is important throughout this skill.
keyPoints: Face high end of wedge
Place hands on mat
Jump off both feet into forward roll
Control throughout
Finish standing, arms by ears
skills.spotting: A spotter may be used if needed.
photoSequence: Coach supporting gymnast at top of wedge, about to roll
Gymnast in tucked roll, coach guiding

skills.name: Forward Roll
skills.description: From a stand, squat down to place the hands on the floor, tuck the head, and perform a forward roll. The back should roll on the floor smoothly from neck to buttocks. Finish in a squat position, and immediately extend the legs to stand. The roll can also be finished in a walkout by keeping one leg tucked while the other extends forward slightly as the body rolls along the back. Again, finish by rising to a stand. Arms are stretched upward near the ears at the end of the skill.
keyPoints: Squat to place hands, tuck head
Back rolls smoothly neck to buttocks
Finish in squat then extend to stand
Can also finish as walkout with one leg extended
Arms stretched by ears at end
photoSequence: Squat position, hands on floor, head tucked
Mid-roll: on upper back, hips overhead, knees tucked

skills.name: Straddle Forward Roll
skills.description: From a straddle stand, pike and place the hands on the floor slightly in front of the feet. Roll over forward, maintaining a straddle position, and push on the floor between the legs to rise to a straight-leg straddle stand. Arms can be in front, slightly to the sides, or stretched upward at the finish.
keyPoints: Straddle stand to start
Pike and place hands in front of feet
Maintain straddle throughout roll
Push between legs to rise
Arms variable at finish
photoSequence: Straddle stand, piking forward, hands reaching to floor
Mid-roll: inverted straddle, back on floor

skills.name: Pike Tuck Forward Roll
skills.description: From a stretched standing position with legs together and arms stretched up next to the ears, pike forward at the hips to place the hands on the floor in front of the feet. Round the back and tuck the head to roll over forward. Tuck the body when the hips touch the floor. Finish in a stand with the arms stretched overhead.
keyPoints: Start standing, arms by ears
Pike at hips to place hands in front of feet
Round back, tuck head
Tuck body when hips contact floor
Finish standing, arms overhead
photoSequence: Standing start, arms high, beginning to pike forward
Hands on floor in front of feet, hips high, ready to roll

skills.name: Half Handstand Forward Roll
skills.description: From a stretched standing position with arms stretched overhead or outward, move through a scale (left or right foot), simultaneously place the hands on the floor, and roll forward, finishing in a stand. Arms are stretched overhead at the finish. The support leg remains in contact with the floor until the back touches on the forward roll.
keyPoints: Start standing, arms overhead
Move through a scale position
Place hands on floor simultaneously
Support leg stays on floor until back contacts
Roll forward, finish standing, arms overhead
photoSequence: Scale position, hands reaching toward floor
Hands on floor, back leg still extended — about to roll
Forward roll completing, rising to stand

skills.name: Handstand Forward Roll Over and Down Incline
skills.description: Place the hands on the floor by the high end of the wedge. Push the feet into a handstand, keeping the arms extended, and lean forward to perform a forward roll over the high edge and down the slope. Finish in a stretched standing position with the arms stretched overhead.
keyPoints: Hands by high end of wedge
Kick to handstand, arms extended
Lean forward to roll over high edge
Finish standing, arms overhead
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended. Assist by holding the gymnast's legs or hips.
photoSequence: Coach supporting gymnast in handstand at top of wedge
Gymnast tipping into forward roll over high edge of wedge

skills.name: Handstand Forward Roll
skills.description: Lunge forward, bend at the hips, and place the hands on the floor. Push up through a handstand position, tuck the head, and lower the upper back and shoulders. Roll forward to finish in a stand with the arms stretched overhead.
keyPoints: Lunge entry
Push up through handstand
Tuck head, lower upper back and shoulders
Roll forward to standing finish
Arms stretched overhead at end
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended. Assist by holding the legs or hips as the gymnast kicks up to handstand. Guide the gymnast gently downward into the forward roll.
photoSequence: Coach supporting gymnast's lunge entry into handstand
Handstand position, coach guiding
Beginning to tip into forward roll, head tucking

skills.name: Rocking
skills.description: From a tuck sit, roll backward until the shoulders touch the floor and the buttocks is raised off the floor, maintaining a tuck position. Practice placing the hands on the floor by the shoulders, with palms down and fingers pointing toward the body. Roll back to the start position.
keyPoints: Start in tuck sit
Roll back to shoulders, buttocks up
Hands placed by shoulders, palms down, fingers toward body
Roll back to start
photoSequence: Tuck sit position, beginning to roll back
On upper back, hips raised, hands pressing on floor beside head

skills.name: Candlestick
skills.description: From a stand, bend the knees to arrive in a tuck sit. Continue rolling backward, and extend the body into a shoulder balance (candlestick), with weight high on the shoulders, toes pointed up, and hip angle as straight as possible. Arms should be straight, with palms on the floor in front of the buttocks.
keyPoints: Stand to tuck sit to rolling back
Extend into shoulder balance — weight high on shoulders
Toes pointed upward
Hip angle as straight as possible
Arms straight, palms on floor in front of buttocks
photoSequence: Tuck sit, beginning to roll back
Shoulder balance: body extended vertically, toes pointed up, arms supporting on floor

skills.name: Back Roll Down Incline
skills.description: Sit on the high end of the wedge facing away from the down slope. Roll backward with the hands beside the head. Hold the legs in a tuck position, using the hands for support as the body becomes inverted. Finish by pushing off the hands to land on the feet in a tuck position and then extending the legs to stand. Arms can be stretched upward or forward.
keyPoints: Sit at high end, facing away from slope
Hands beside head throughout
Tuck legs during inversion
Push off hands to land on feet
Extend legs to stand at finish
photoSequence: Seated at top of wedge, beginning to roll back
Inverted on wedge, tucked, hands pushing off

skills.name: Back Straddle Roll Down Incline
skills.description: Sit on the high end of the wedge facing away from the down slope. Roll backward, separating the legs, with the hands beside the head. Straighten the legs to a straddle position, using the hands for support. Finish by pushing off the hands to a straddle stand.
keyPoints: Sit at high end, facing away from slope
Roll back, separate legs to straddle
Hands beside head
Straighten legs in straddle during inversion
Push off hands to straddle stand
photoSequence: Seated at high end, beginning to roll back
Inverted straddle on wedge, hands pushing off
Landing in straddle stand at bottom

skills.name: Back Roll Down Incline to Stretch Jump
skills.description: Perform a back roll down the wedge to a stand. Concentrate on rolling in a straight line. As the legs are extending to the standing position, jump upward with arms stretched overhead and land on the mat on two feet showing control.
keyPoints: Back roll down wedge to stand
Roll in a straight line
As legs extend to stand, immediately jump upward
Arms stretched overhead in jump
Land on two feet with control
photoSequence: Rising from back roll into standing position
Immediately jumping upward, arms overhead

skills.name: Straddle Tuck Backward Roll
skills.description: From a straddle stand, roll backward while simultaneously reaching between the legs for assistance. Continue rolling backward, using the arms to support the body, and bring the knees together. Finish in a squat stand with the arms to the sides or straight out in front of the body.
keyPoints: Straddle stand start
Roll back while reaching between legs
Arms support body during inversion
Knees come together
Finish in squat stand, arms to sides or forward
photoSequence: Straddle stand, reaching between legs to roll back
Inverted, rolling through, knees drawing together
Finishing in squat stand

skills.name: Straddle Backward Roll
skills.description: From a straddle position, either standing or sitting, roll backward while maintaining a straddle position. Push off the hands, finishing in a straddle stand with the arms stretched upward by the ears.
keyPoints: Straddle start (standing or sitting)
Maintain straddle throughout roll
Push off hands
Finish in straddle stand, arms by ears
photoSequence: Straddle sit or stand, beginning to roll backward
Inverted straddle, hands pushing off floor
Straddle stand finish, arms by ears

skills.name: Backward Roll
skills.description: From a stretched standing position, perform a backward roll to a stretched standing position with the arms stretched straight up by the ears.
keyPoints: Start and finish in stretched standing position
Arms stretched up by ears at finish
photoSequence: Standing start, beginning to sit and roll back
On upper back, tucked, hands pressing by head

skills.name: Pike Tuck Backward Roll
skills.description: Begin in a pike standing position, bend forward, and reach backward to catch the body's weight with the hands as the roll is initiated. As the buttocks touches the floor, continue to roll backward by tucking the knees and the arms up beside the head. Push off the hands, moving through a squat position and finishing in a stand with the arms stretched upward.
keyPoints: Start in pike stand
Reach backward to catch weight as roll initiates
Tuck knees and arms beside head as buttocks contacts
Push off hands through squat to stand
Arms stretched upward at finish
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended. Lift the gymnast at the hips when assisting.
photoSequence: Pike stand, beginning to reach back to floor
Buttocks on floor, catching weight with hands, beginning to tuck
Tucked backward roll, hands pushing off

skills.name: Tuck Pike Backward Roll
skills.description: From a squat position, roll backward and straighten the legs to finish in a pike to stand with the arms stretched upward at the end of the skill.
keyPoints: Start in squat
Roll backward
Straighten legs during roll to pike
Finish in pike to stand
Arms stretched upward
photoSequence: Squat position, rolling backward
On back, legs extending from tuck to pike
Finishing in pike position, rising to stand

skills.name: Backward Roll With Jumps
skills.description: Perform jumps in various positions before and after a backward roll. Show balance before rolling backward, support on the hands during the roll, and control during the final jump and landing. Arms are stretched upward at the completion of the skills.
keyPoints: Perform jumps before and after the backward roll
Show balance before rolling
Support on hands during roll
Control during final jump and landing
Arms stretched upward at completion
photoSequence: Standing tall, arms overhead — pre-roll jump
Jump in pike or straddle position
Landing and immediately sitting into backward roll
On back, tucked, hands pushing
Rising from roll
Post-roll jump, arms overhead

skills.name: Backward Roll With Straight Arms to Pike Stand
skills.description: From a squat position, roll backward. Maintaining straight arms and an inward-facing hand position, reach backward so that the sides of the little fingers contact the floor first behind the head. As the hips roll backward through vertical, press down against the floor with arms as straight as possible. Extend the legs into a pike position to finish in a straight stand with arms stretched overhead.
keyPoints: Squat start
Straight arms throughout — no bend
Inward-facing hand position, little fingers contact first
Press down through vertical
Extend legs to pike to finish in straight stand
photoSequence: Hips inverted, straight arms pressing into floor, body nearing vertical
Pike position as body passes vertical, legs extending

skills.name: Backward Roll to Front Support
skills.description: Perform a backward roll with straight arms. As the hips are rolling over the head, extend the legs to a hollow body position, and maintain this position to finish in a push-up position (front support) with legs together and balls of the feet on the floor.
keyPoints: Backward roll with straight arms
As hips pass over head, extend legs to hollow body
Maintain hollow position
Finish in front support — legs together, on balls of feet
photoSequence: Hips over head, arms pressing, legs beginning to extend
Front support (plank) position — straight body, weight on hands and toes

skills.name: Low Back Extension
skills.description: Sit on the high end of the wedge. Roll backward down the wedge, placing the hands by the shoulders. Extend at the hips and knees as the hands contact the wedge, and extend the arms to lift the shoulders off the wedge. Finish by stepping down one leg at a time to a lunge and extending the arms overhead.
keyPoints: Sit at high end, roll backward down wedge
Place hands by shoulders
Extend hips and knees as hands contact wedge
Extend arms to lift shoulders
Step down one leg at a time to lunge finish
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended. Lift the gymnast at the legs or hips when assisting.
photoSequence: Coach supporting gymnast in bridge position on wedge, arms extended
Gymnast stepping down to lunge, coach assisting

skills.name: Hand-Hand-Foot-Foot
skills.description: Begin standing in a straddle position on a half circle of rope, facing the center of the half circle. Perform a cartwheel-type movement around the rope by moving sideward, hand-hand-foot-foot (cartwheel action), finishing in a straddle standing position. Arms are stretched straight overhead at the completion. Repeat the activity in the opposite direction.
keyPoints: Straddle stand on half circle of rope, facing center
Cartwheel action — hand, hand, foot, foot
Move sideward around the rope
Arms straight overhead at finish
Repeat in opposite direction
photoSequence: Straddle stand on rope, arms raised, beginning cartwheel action
Inverted position with hands on floor (or mat), legs in cartwheel

skills.name: Half Cartwheel Over Mats
skills.description: From a straddle stand perpendicular to a folded panel mat, place the hands on the mat using a cartwheel action to move to the other side of the mat. Arms are stretched upward and slightly outward at the completion. Repeat the activity with the other foot first.
keyPoints: Straddle stand perpendicular to folded panel mat
Cartwheel action to cross the mat
Arms stretched upward and slightly outward at finish
Repeat with other foot leading
photoSequence: Straddle stand beside mat, arms raised, initiating cartwheel
Inverted over mat, hands on surface, legs in cartwheel split

skills.name: Low Cartwheel Over Rope
skills.description: Reach out and over a rope (12 to 24 inches [30 to 60 cm] high), placing the hands on a mat, and push off one leg at a time using the cartwheel action to finish in a straddle stand on the other side of the rope. Arms are stretched upward at the completion. Repeat the activity on the other side.
keyPoints: Reach over rope 30–60 cm high
Hands on mat, push off one leg at a time
Cartwheel action
Finish in straddle stand on other side
Arms stretched upward at finish
photoSequence: Reaching over rope, hands going to mat on other side
Inverted cartwheel over rope, legs in straddle

skills.name: Cartwheel
skills.description: From a straddle stand with arms stretched outward, perform a cartwheel. Finish the cartwheel in a side stand, and progress to a lunge. Arms are stretched upward and slightly outward at the completion. Repeat the activity on both sides.
keyPoints: Straddle stand start, arms stretched outward
Cartwheel action — hand, hand, foot, foot
Finish in side stand progressing to lunge
Arms stretched upward at finish
Practise on both sides
skills.spotting: Spotting is recommended on the first few attempts. Assist the gymnast by lifting at the hips.
photoSequence: Straddle stand, arms out, beginning cartwheel
Inverted cartwheel: hands on floor, legs in wide straddle
Finishing to side stand/lunge, arms overhead

skills.name: Three Steps to Cartwheel
skills.description: Take three walking steps, raising the arms during the third step, and perform a cartwheel. Finish the cartwheel in a side stand, and progress to a lunge. Arms are stretched upward and slightly outward at the completion. Repeat on the other side.
keyPoints: Three walking steps
Raise arms on third step
Cartwheel from step
Finish in side stand to lunge
Repeat on both sides
photoSequence: Third step of approach, arms raising
Inverted cartwheel: hands on floor, legs splitting

skills.name: Power Hurdle Cartwheel
skills.description: Jump from two feet, stretching tall. Come down on one foot, with the other foot reaching forward in a lunge position. Arms should be up by the ears, and the body should be leaning slightly forward. Pass through the lunge position to perform a cartwheel. Arms are stretched upward and slightly outward at the completion.
keyPoints: Jump from two feet, stretch tall
Land on one foot with other reaching forward — lunge
Arms by ears, body leaning slightly forward
Pass through lunge into cartwheel
Arms stretched upward at finish
photoSequence: Two-foot jump, stretching tall
Landing in lunge — arms high, slight forward lean
Cartwheel: inverted, hands on floor, legs splitting

skills.name: Cartwheel-Style Round-Off
skills.description: Step forward to place the hands on the floor and perform a cartwheel. As the first leg contacts the floor, turn the body 90 degrees to face the starting point, and close the second leg to the first to finish with feet together in a stand. Arms are stretched overhead at the completion.
keyPoints: Step forward, cartwheel entry
As first leg lands, rotate body 90 degrees to face start
Close second leg to first — feet together
Arms stretched overhead at finish
photoSequence: Inverted cartwheel position, about to land first leg
First leg landing, body rotating to face start direction
Both feet together, standing, arms overhead

skills.name: Step Into Round-Off
skills.description: Step forward through a lunge with a strong push off the front leg. Place the hands on the floor. As the feet pass overhead (through vertical), push off the hands, turn 90 degrees, and close the legs together quickly, just before the first foot contacts the floor. Finish with the knees slightly flexed and, if possible, the body leaning backward. Arms are stretched overhead at the completion.
keyPoints: Lunge entry, strong push off front leg
Hands on floor
Feet pass through vertical
Push off hands and rotate 90 degrees
Close legs before first foot lands
Knees slightly flexed, body leaning back
Arms overhead
photoSequence: Lunge entry, hands going to floor
Inverted, legs passing through vertical
Rotating, legs closing, about to land
Two-foot landing, knees flexed, body leaning back

skills.name: 40-Foot Run
skills.description: Run down a vault runway or flat, open surface for approximately 40 feet (12 m). Vary speed with each run, moving from slow to medium to fast.
keyPoints: 40 feet (12 m) distance
Vary speed: slow to medium to fast
photoSequence: Running technique: heels lift toward buttocks, front knee raises to horizontal, body leaning slightly forward, arms bent at approximately 90 degrees pumping close to sides

skills.name: High Knees
skills.description: Run with shorter strides and an upright torso (little to no forward lean), raising the thighs to horizontal or above with each step.
keyPoints: Shorter strides than normal running
Upright torso, little to no forward lean
Raise thighs to horizontal or above with each step
photoSequence: Running with exaggerated knee lift: front thigh raised to horizontal, upright torso

skills.name: Rear Kicks
skills.description: Run with shorter strides and an upright torso (little to no forward lean), bending the knees to full flexion, with the heels coming up toward the buttocks.
keyPoints: Shorter strides
Upright torso
Bend knees to full flexion
Heels kick up toward buttocks
photoSequence: Running with heels kicking to buttocks on each stride, upright posture

skills.name: Deer Leaps
skills.description: Run with extra-long strides.
keyPoints: Extra-long strides
photoSequence: Running with exaggerated long stride, extended flight phase between footfalls

skills.name: One-Leg Run
skills.description: Perform a hopping motion on one leg, using the arms and the free leg to generate additional speed and power. Repeat on the other leg.
keyPoints: Hop on one leg repeatedly
Use arms and free leg to generate speed and power
Repeat on other leg
photoSequence: One-leg hopping run: bounding on single leg with arms pumping and free leg driving

skills.name: Jump On Target
skills.description: Take one step, lift one leg forward and upward, push off the other leg, and bring both legs together in the air. Land with feet together in the hoop (on target). Body should be upright, with knees flexed slightly and in front of shoulders. Arms remain in front middle position or can be stretched upward.
keyPoints: One step, lift one leg forward and up
Push off other leg, bring legs together in air
Land on target with feet together
Body upright, knees slightly flexed in front of shoulders
Arms front middle or stretched upward
photoSequence: One step, leading leg lifting, push-off leg extending
Both feet together in air above hoop
Landing in hoop, upright, knees slightly bent

skills.name: Arm Circle
skills.description: Standing in place, practice the underarm swing used with the hurdle. Move arms in a small, quick circle from the sides through backward low position to finish in front. Repeat.
keyPoints: Practice the hurdle arm action in place
Small quick circle
Arms move from sides, through backward low, to front
Repeat multiple times
photoSequence: Arms beginning backward swing from sides
Arms completing circle to front middle position

skills.name: Hurdle On Target
skills.description: Take one step, lift one leg forward and upward, push off the other leg, and bring both legs together in the air. Simultaneously circle the arms backward. Finish by landing on both feet in the hoop (on target), with the arms at front middle position.
keyPoints: Same as jump on target but add arm circle
Arms circle backward simultaneously with jump
Land on both feet in target
Arms at front middle on landing
photoSequence: One step, leading leg up, arms starting backward circle
Both feet in air, arms completing circle
Landing in hoop, arms at front middle

skills.name: Run, Hurdle, Rebound, Stretch Jump
skills.description: From a short run (seven to nine steps), perform a hurdle onto the springboard, rebound, and perform a stretch jump to a controlled landing position on the mat. Arms are at front middle on landing.
keyPoints: 7–9 step run
Hurdle onto springboard
Rebound and perform stretch jump
Controlled landing position on mat
Arms at front middle on landing
skills.spotting: An 8-inch (20 cm) mat is recommended for landing.
photoSequence: Hurdle onto springboard, body nearly vertical on contact
Airborne in stretch jump off springboard
Controlled landing on mat

skills.name: Run, Hurdle, Rebound
skills.description: Make the previous drill more difficult by replacing the stretch jump with other types of jumps such as a tuck jump, straddle jump, or stretch jump with 180-degree turn, and so on.
keyPoints: Same as run-hurdle-rebound-stretch-jump
Replace stretch jump with other jump varieties: tuck, straddle, 180-degree turn
photoSequence: Hurdle onto springboard
Airborne in tuck jump (or other jump type)
Landing on mat

skills.name: Traveling Jumps Up and Down
skills.description: Travel forward on a mat (folded panel or otherwise) with support on the hands by jumping from the side of the mat to the top of the mat, to the other side of the mat, back to the top, and so forth. Each time the feet have support on the floor, move the hands forward 6 to 12 inches (15 to 30 cm).
keyPoints: Travel forward on mat with hand support
Jump: floor side → top → other side → top etc.
Move hands forward 6–12 inches each time feet land on floor
photoSequence: Hands on mat edge, feet on floor to one side
Feet jumping to top of mat, hands on mat surface
Feet on floor other side of mat, hands advancing forward

skills.name: Jump-Jump-Stretch Jump
skills.description: Run, hurdle, and rebound off the board, landing on the first folded panel mat. Then jump from the first mat to the second mat, and jump off the second mat showing a stretched position. Execute a safe landing position (knees slightly bent, torso hollowed, arms at front middle). Maintain balance and control throughout the drill, keeping the legs together.
keyPoints: Run, hurdle, rebound off board
Land on first mat, jump to second mat
Jump off second mat in stretched position
Safe landing: knees slightly bent, torso hollowed, arms at front middle
Legs together throughout
photoSequence: Rebounding off springboard, landing on first mat
Jumping from first mat to second, stretched
Stretch jump off second mat
Safe landing position

skills.name: Traveling Jumps Over and Back
skills.description: Travel forward on a mat (folded panel or otherwise) with support on the hands by jumping from one side of the mat to the other without touching the feet on the top of the mat, raising the arms for each landing.
keyPoints: Travel forward on mat with hand support
Jump directly from one side to other — no touching top of mat
Raise arms for each landing
photoSequence: Hands on mat, feet on floor one side
Jumping over mat, hands advancing
Landing other side, arms raising

skills.name: Jump-Jump Series
skills.description: Using the technique for the jump-jump-stretch jump, make the drill progressively more difficult by replacing the final jump with other types of jumps such as a tuck jump, straddle jump, pike jump, stretch jump with 180-degree turn, and so on.
keyPoints: Same setup as jump-jump-stretch-jump
Replace final jump with increasingly difficult jump types
photoSequence: Rebounding off board to first mat
Jump to second mat
Advanced jump (tuck/straddle/pike) off second mat
Safe landing

skills.name: Triple Jump to Forward Roll
skills.description: Run, hurdle, and rebound off the board, landing on the first mat. Then jump from the first mat to the second mat, showing a stretched position, and land on the feet in demi-plié with a slight forward body lean. Reach forward, tuck the head, and do a forward roll to stand. Finish in a stretched standing position with arms overhead. Jumps should be done with good balance and control.
keyPoints: Run, hurdle, rebound to first mat
Jump first to second mat in stretched position
Land in demi-plié with slight forward lean
Reach forward, tuck head into forward roll
Finish in stretched stand, arms overhead
Balance and control throughout
photoSequence: Rebounding from board to first mat
Jumping from first to second mat
Landing in demi-plié, beginning to pitch forward
Forward roll on mat
Rising to stretched stand

skills.name: Stretch Jump and Stick
skills.description: From a stand on the vault surface (mat stack or vault table), perform a stretch jump to a safe landing position. Stick the safe landing position with arms at front middle. Reinforce holding the stuck landing position for two or three seconds.
keyPoints: Jump from raised vault surface
Stretch jump to safe landing position
Arms at front middle on landing
Hold stuck landing for 2–3 seconds
photoSequence: Standing on raised mat stack, jumping off
Airborne in stretch jump
Stuck landing: feet together, knees bent, arms at front middle

skills.name: Squat Jumps in a Series
skills.description: From a squat position on the floor, lean forward and push off the legs while reaching forward with the hands. With momentary support on the hands, bring the knees forward to return to a squat position. Repeat for a series of three to five jumps.
keyPoints: Start in squat
Push off legs, reach forward with hands
Momentary hand support
Bring knees forward to return to squat
Repeat 3–5 times
photoSequence: Squat position on floor, about to push off
Briefly on hands, legs drawn up in tuck

skills.name: Squat Thrusts in a Series
skills.description: From a front support position on the floor, shift the weight to the hands, and bring the knees forward to a squat position. Then return to a front support. Repeat the activity three to five times.
keyPoints: Start in front support (plank)
Shift weight to hands, bring knees to squat
Return to front support
Repeat 3–5 times
photoSequence: Front support (plank) position
Knees drawn forward to squat position, weight on hands

skills.name: Squat On, Bounce, Squat On
skills.description: From a squat position on top of a stack of mats, shift the weight onto the hands, extend the legs backward, and snap the legs down to the board, keeping the hands down on the stacked mats during the foot contact with the board. Rebound off the board, and return to a squat position on the mats.
keyPoints: Squat on mat stack
Shift weight to hands, extend legs back to board
Keep hands on mats during foot contact with board
Rebound back to squat on mats
photoSequence: Squat on mat stack, hands on surface
Legs extended back, feet on board, hands still on mats
Rebounding back to squat on mats

skills.name: Squat On, Forward Roll
skills.description: With the springboard close to a stack of mats, stand on the springboard with the hands on the mat stack and bounce from the board, raising the hips to a squat position on top of the mats. Forward roll on top of the stacked mats to a stand on the mats. Optional: After the forward roll, perform a stretch jump from the mats to the floor with a controlled landing.
keyPoints: Springboard close to mat stack
Hands on mats, bounce from board to squat on top of mats
Forward roll on top of mats to stand
Optional: stretch jump off mats to floor
photoSequence: Hands on mat, bouncing from springboard, hips rising
Squat position on top of mats
Forward roll on mat surface

skills.name: Bounce, Squat On, Jump Off
skills.description: From a stand on the springboard with the hands on top of a stack of mats, bounce from the board to a squat on the mats. Immediately jump forward off the mats with a controlled landing.
keyPoints: Stand on springboard, hands on mat stack
Bounce from board to squat on mats
Immediately jump forward off mats
Controlled landing
photoSequence: Bouncing from springboard to squat on mat stack
Squat position on mats, about to jump
Jumping forward off mats, stretch jump to landing

skills.name: Squat On, Jump Off
skills.description: Walk (or run) forward three to five steps, hurdle, and rebound off the board with two feet. Vault to a squat position on the mats and immediately jump forward off the mats with a controlled landing.
keyPoints: 3–5 step walk or run
Hurdle and two-foot rebound
Vault to squat on mats
Immediately jump forward
Controlled landing
photoSequence: Hurdling and rebounding off springboard
Squatting onto mat stack
Jumping forward off mats to landing

skills.name: Squat Vault
skills.description: Run three to five steps, hurdle, and rebound off the springboard with two feet. Using an underarm lift, reach forward with the arms to a front support, with the legs extended to the back and the hips slightly above the shoulders. Simultaneously push off the mats with both hands and tuck the knees. The shoulders rise with the push from the mats, and the head and torso are vertical as the body passes over the mats. Extend the legs to land in a controlled position.
keyPoints: 3–5 step run, hurdle, two-foot rebound
Underarm lift — reach forward to front support
Legs back, hips slightly above shoulders
Simultaneous hand push and knee tuck
Shoulders rise with push
Head and torso vertical as body passes over
Extend legs to land
photoSequence: Hurdle and springboard rebound
Front support on vault, hips above shoulders
Pushing off, tucking knees over vault
Landing in controlled position

skills.name: Donkey Kicks
skills.description: While standing on the board, place the hands on the stacked mats and bounce on the board, driving the heels back and up into the air toward a handstand position. Upon landing, immediately spring back up. Repeat numerous times, keeping the head neutral and working to achieve a straight, slightly hollow body shape. This drill can be done in the tuck position, with progression to a pike, and then finally with straight legs.
keyPoints: Stand on board, hands on mats
Bounce on board, drive heels back and up toward handstand
Land and immediately spring back up
Head neutral throughout
Aim for straight, slightly hollow body
Progress: tuck → pike → straight legs
photoSequence: Hands on mat, feet on springboard, kicking heels upward
Near-handstand position, heels driven high

skills.name: Handstand Fall to Back
skills.description: Move through a lunge position to kick up to a handstand position on the stacked mats. Pass through vertical and land flat on the back on a skill cushion (at least 8 inches [20 cm]). Maintain a straight, tight body with the head in neutral position throughout.
keyPoints: Lunge entry, kick to handstand on mats
Pass through vertical
Land flat on back on skill cushion
Straight, tight body throughout
Head neutral
photoSequence: Lunge approach, kicking to handstand on mat stack
Handstand position, passing through vertical
Falling to flat back on skill cushion

skills.name: Handstand Hops
skills.description: Passing through a lunge position, swing the arms from backward low to forward high, place the hands on the floor, and kick up to a handstand. Keeping the arms straight, extend the shoulders quickly, creating a push (block) off the floor to perform small hops on the hands. Keep the body straight and tight, with legs together. Step down to a lunge position.
keyPoints: Lunge entry, arm swing from backward low to forward high
Kick to handstand, arms straight
Extend shoulders quickly to block off floor
Small hops on hands
Body straight and tight, legs together
Step down to lunge
photoSequence: Lunge approach, arms swinging forward high
Handstand position, performing shoulder block
Small hop off hands

skills.name: Handstand Block to Back
skills.description: Take one to three steps, swing the arms from backward low to forward middle, place the hands on the springboard while moving through a lunge, and kick up to a handstand. The arms remain straight, and the shoulders extend quickly so that the hands block off the board. Show flight through vertical, with the body rotating to land in a straight hollow lying position on a skill cushion (at least 16 inches [40 cm]). The head is neutral throughout, the body is tight, and the legs are together.
keyPoints: 1–3 steps, arm swing backward low to forward middle
Hands on springboard through lunge
Kick to handstand, arms straight
Shoulders extend quickly — block off board
Flight through vertical
Rotate to land in straight hollow lying position on thick skill cushion
Head neutral, body tight, legs together
photoSequence: 1–3 step approach, arms swinging
Hands on springboard through lunge, kicking to handstand
Handstand over springboard, blocking with shoulders
Flight through vertical
Flat hollow landing on thick skill cushion

skills.name: Dive Forward Roll Onto Mats
skills.description: From a short run (seven to nine steps), hurdle onto the board and rebound, extending the legs and reaching forward to place the hands on the mats. Maintain a hollow body position while lifting the hips over the shoulders and head. Upon contact with the mats, bend the arms slightly, tuck the chin, and perform a tucked forward roll onto the mats.
keyPoints: 7–9 step run, hurdle, rebound
Extend legs and reach forward to place hands on mats
Hollow body position, hips over shoulders and head
On mat contact: arms bend slightly, chin tucks
Tucked forward roll onto mats
skills.spotting: The mat stack should be waist height for the gymnast, including a skill cushion on top.
photoSequence: Hurdling and rebounding off springboard, reaching forward
Hollow body flight, hips rising
Hands contacting mat, body going into tucked forward roll
Rolling through on mats
